Title: Convergent evolution of the UbiA prenyltransferase family underlies the independent acquisition of furanocoumarins in plants

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: UbiA prenyltransferase superfamily; aromatic prenyltransferase with multiple transmembrane helices, two Asp-rich motifs, N-terminal transit peptide
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: linear furanocoumarin pathway; first committed prenylation step from simple coumarin precursor
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: committed step; upstream entry step into furanocoumarin pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a dimethylallyl group from DMAPP to umbelliferone at C6 to form dimethylsuberosin (DMS); also dimethylallylates 5-methoxy-7-hydroxycoumarin in vitro
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate committed precursor toward linear furanocoumarins
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: potent allelochemical/defence metabolites; phototoxic and broadly toxic to herbivores, fungi and other organisms
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: latex, especially trunk and petiole latex
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expressed in latexes from fruits, petioles and trunks; expression pattern related to furanocoumarin accumulation across latex samples
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: expressed in latexes from fruits, petioles and trunks
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: comparative RNA-seq candidate discovery; RT-PCR cloning; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; microsome in vitro enzyme assay; LC/MS product identification; kinetic analysis; qRT-PCR; subcellular localization with transit peptide-sGFP
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: catalyzes the first committed step of furanocoumarin biosynthesis in fig and thereby promotes formation of furanocoumarin precursors
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FcPT1 was identified from fig latex transcriptomes as the principal UDT candidate. 2. Recombinant FcPT1 converted umbelliferone plus DMAPP into dimethylsuberosin, confirming U6DT activity. 3. FcPT1 showed narrow substrate specificity, preferring umbelliferone and DMAPP. 4. FcPT1 localized to plastids/chloroplasts. 5. Phylogeny and exon structure indicate FcPT1 evolved independently from Apiaceae UDTs, from a VTE2-2-related ancestor.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: first committed step; key entry enzyme into furanocoumarin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of fig latexes; accession NA in text
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: FcPT1a and FcPT1b are allelic variants mapped to the same genomic locus (BDEM01000717.1). In vitro, FcPT1 did not produce osthenol unlike some Apiaceae U6DTs. The study focuses mainly on FcPT1a after both variants showed equivalent activity.

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: UbiA family prenyltransferase of unknown function
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: UbiA prenyltransferase superfamily; multiple transmembrane helices; N-terminal transit peptide; atypical first Asp-rich motif substitution
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: No detectable activity with tested substrate combinations including umbelliferone plus DMAPP under assay conditions
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: FcPT2-related contigs present in latex transcriptomes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-PCR cloning; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; microsome enzyme assay showing no product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: no proven role in furanocoumarin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FcPT2a and FcPT2b were cloned as additional UbiA candidates from fig latex. 2. Recombinant FcPT2a/b showed no detectable activity with tested coumarin/prenyl donor combinations including umbelliferone and DMAPP. 3. FcPT2 was therefore not validated as the fig UDT involved in furanocoumarin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of fig latexes; accession NA in text
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as an experimentally tested core candidate in Results, but function remained unresolved; allelic variants map to scaffold BDEM01000270.1.
